2604.07615.title: ADAG: Automatically Describing Attribution Graphs
2604.07615.paper_summary: In language model interpretability research, \textbf{circuit tracing} aims to identify which internal features causally contributed to a particular output and how they affected each other, with the goal of explaining the computations underlying some behaviour. However, all prior circuit tracing work has relied on ad-hoc human interpretation of the role that each feature in the circuit plays, via manual inspection of data artifacts such as the dataset examples the component activates on. We introduce \textbf{ADAG}, an end-to-end pipeline for describing these attribution graphs which is fully automated. To achieve this, we introduce \textit{attribution profiles} which quantify the functional role of a feature via its input and output gradient effects. We then introduce a novel clustering algorithm for grouping features, and an LLM explainer--simulator setup which generates and scores natural-language explanations of the functional role of these feature groups. We run our system on known human-analysed circuit-tracing tasks and recover interpretable circuits, and further show ADAG can find steerable clusters which are responsible for a harmful advice jailbreak in Llama 3.1 8B Instruct.
2604.07615.paper_summary_zh: 在語言模型可解釋性研究中，\textbf{電路追蹤}旨在識別哪些內部特徵對特定輸出有因果貢獻，以及它們如何相互影響，目的是解釋某些行為背後的計算。然而，所有先前的電路追蹤工作都依賴於對電路中每個特徵角色的臨時人為解釋，通過手動檢查數據工件，例如該組件在其上激活的數據集示例。我們介紹了\textbf{ADAG}，這是一個完全自動化的端到端管道，用於描述這些歸因圖。為了實現這一點，我們引入了\textit{歸因配置文件}，它通過特徵的輸入和輸出梯度效應來量化特徵的功能角色。然後，我們介紹了一種新穎的聚類算法來分組特徵，以及一個LLM解釋器-模擬器設置，該設置生成並評分這些特徵組的功能角色的自然語言解釋。我們在已知的人類分析電路追蹤任務上運行我們的系統，並恢復可解釋的電路，進一步顯示ADAG可以找到負責Llama 3.1 8B Instruct中有害建議越獄的可操控集群。
2604.07615.author: Aryaman Arora et.al.
2604.07615.authors: Aryaman Arora, Zhengxuan Wu, Jacob Steinhardt, Sarah Schwettmann
